title: The Century Report: March 23 2026
description:
TCR 03/23/26: Batteries Built in Months

Read the full edition of today's TCR here: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-23-2026/

U.S. grid battery manufacturing crossed the self-sufficiency threshold yesterday - from effectively zero domestic lithium iron phosphate cell capacity eighteen months ago to a projected 96 gigawatt-hours by year's end, driven by policy incentives and EV manufacturers retooling idle plants for the surging grid storage market. Earendil Labs raised $787 million backed by Sanofi and Pfizer for an AI biologics platform that has generated over 40 drug programs in under a year, while Heidelberg University researchers identified a toxic NMDAR/TRPM4 protein complex that kills neurons in Alzheimer's-affected brains through a mechanism distinct from amyloid plaques and developed a compound that disrupted it, preserving learning and memory in mice. NIH-supported researchers published a four-marker blood test detecting pancreatic cancer in over 90% of cases including early-stage tumors, and Ann Arbor launched the first community-owned clean energy utility in the country with 80% voter approval. Cursor confirmed its flagship Composer 2 model was built on Moonshot AI's open-source Kimi 2.5 base without initial disclosure, and Senator Warren sent formal letters demanding full military AI contract details and calling the Anthropic supply-chain risk designation "apparent retaliation."

The Century Report, by Shared Sapience, is a relentlessly optimistic daily briefing on AI, science, energy, and the systems reshaping civilization - grounded by rigorous evidence from the stories of exponential progress happening every single day.
